summary: Fighter cleanup pass that introduced a C implementation of Fighter_SetupParts in src/melee/ft/ftparts.c, renamed the old func_800743E0 symbol to Fighter_SetupParts across assembly, declarations, and Fighter_Create, renamed lbRefract func_800226A8 to lbRefract_PObjLoad, and moved the func_80081298 declaration from fighter.h to ftcliffcommon.h. The new Fighter_SetupParts code documents and reproduces the fighter JObj traversal used to populate FighterBone entries and count DObjs, with explicit bounds/mismatch asserts against ftPartsTable parts_num.
what_changed: Added new source file src/melee/ft/ftparts.c containing Fighter_SetupParts(HSD_GObj* fighter_obj).
Renamed func_800743E0 to Fighter_SetupParts in asm/melee/ft/ftparts.s, include/functions.h, asm/melee/ft/ft_unknown_006.s, and the Fighter_Create call site in src/melee/ft/fighter.c.
Implemented Fighter_SetupParts as an iterative traversal over a fighter's HSD_JObj hierarchy, filling fighter->x5E8_fighterBones[part] via func_80074194 and tracking fighter->x5EC_dobj_list.count.
Added local ftparts traversal helpers/macros MAX_FT_PARTS, JOBJ_NEXT, JOBJ_PARENT, and JOBJ_CHILD, and extern declarations for func_8007506C and func_80074194.
Added safety checks in Fighter_SetupParts: assert if ftPartsTable[fighter->x4_fighterKind]->parts_num exceeds MAX_FT_PARTS, and assert if the final traversed part count does not equal parts_num.
Renamed lbrefract func_800226A8 to lbRefract_PObjLoad in src/melee/lb/lbrefract.h and asm/melee/lb/lbrefract_002.s, including class-info callback references in ftparts.s and lbrefract_002.s.
Cleaned trailing whitespace on several blr/blrl lines in asm/melee/lb/lbrefract_002.s.
Moved BOOL func_80081298(HSD_GObj* gobj) declaration out of src/melee/ft/fighter.h and into src/melee/ft/ftcliffcommon.h.
Added #include <melee/ft/ftcliffcommon.h> to src/melee/ft/code_80081B38.c so the moved func_80081298 declaration remains available where needed.
smart_moves: Converted the opaque func_800743E0 name to the descriptive Fighter_SetupParts name at both C and assembly call sites, preserving linkage while making the fighter creation path easier to read.
Kept traversal null-safe by wrapping direct HSD_JObj next/parent/child access in JOBJ_* macros that return NULL when the input JObj is NULL.
Encoded the original traversal as an iterative depth-first walk with comments explaining the left descent, bottom-node left-to-right visit, and climb back up to continue right.
Preserved runtime sanity checks from the original logic with OSReport and __assert messages for too many fighter parts and mismatched part counts.
Improved header ownership by relocating func_80081298 from broad fighter.h to ftcliffcommon.h, reducing unrelated exposure in the central fighter header.
Renamed lbRefract_PObjLoad based on its use as a PObj load callback, including references stored into class-info fields, which gives future decomp work a more semantically useful anchor.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling fighter setup code, name functions according to their role in the fighter lifecycle; Fighter_SetupParts is more searchable and maintainable than an address-based func_* name.
Tree traversals over HSD_JObj can be represented in C without recursion by tracking current jobj, tree_depth, and explicit next/parent/child transitions.

For matching old code that walks object trees, preserve the exact order of traversal and side effects: skipped parts still increment part, func_80074194 receives the current bone, current jobj, dobj_count pointer, and tree_depth, and the final dobj_count is written back to the fighter.
Header declarations should live with the subsystem that owns the behavior; cliff-common functions should be declared in ftcliffcommon.h rather than fighter.h.
Renaming an assembly symbol requires updating every assembly branch/callback reference and C declaration/call site together to avoid split names.
Callback symbols used in class initialization, such as lbRefract_PObjLoad, are good candidates for semantic naming because their storage location and type hint at their purpose.
naming_conventions: Fighter lifecycle helpers use the Fighter_* prefix, as in Fighter_SetupParts.
Library refract helpers use an lbRefract_* prefix, as in lbRefract_PObjLoad.
Temporary object-tree access helpers are uppercase macro-style names, e.g. JOBJ_NEXT, JOBJ_PARENT, JOBJ_CHILD.
The PR keeps existing address-based func_* names for still-unidentified helpers such as func_8007506C and func_80074194 rather than inventing names without evidence.
Fighter enum constants use FTKIND_* naming, visible in surrounding fighter.h context.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used assembly symbol renames rather than behavior changes for func_800743E0 -> Fighter_SetupParts and func_800226A8 -> lbRefract_PObjLoad.
Updated lis/addi relocation operands in assembly from func_800226A8@ha/@l to lbRefract_PObjLoad@ha/@l where the function pointer is installed.
Maintained branch targets and instruction bytes while changing only symbol names in assembly call sites, e.g. the bl at 800BE9D4 now targets Fighter_SetupParts.
The C Fighter_SetupParts implementation mirrors a depth-first JObj walk using part, tree_depth, and dobj_count locals, likely chosen to match existing codegen/control flow.

The implementation uses explicit NULL checks and continue/break structure to model the original traversal paths: descend child if not JOBJ_INSTANCE, otherwise visit next sibling, otherwise climb parents until a next sibling exists or traversal finishes.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
One review is recorded in the metadata, but no review body or actionable feedback is included in the excerpt.
PR body notes that this includes equivalent commits from PR #636 up to commit 76aae6e1526ea90b8b5717c6f6e5b39668495644 and got stuck on commit 178ba6df7efba665b253ddcc100d74d6ab0ed488.
follow_up_queries: Check PR #636 and commit 76aae6e1526ea90b8b5717c6f6e5b39668495644 for the source of the equivalent fighter cleanup commits referenced in the PR body.
Investigate commit 178ba6df7efba665b253ddcc100d74d6ab0ed488 to understand what blocked further porting in this cleanup pass.
Look for later PRs that further decompile or rename func_80074194 and func_8007506C, since they are central to Fighter_SetupParts.
Search later history for movement of Fighter_SetupParts declaration from include/functions.h into a more specific ftparts header.
Verify whether MAX_FT_PARTS 140 becomes a named constant in a shared fighter parts header in later cleanup work.
